ReviewTrackers positions itself around the statistic that the large majority of consumers use online reviews to guide everyday purchasing decisions, and its target customers are typically multi-location or franchise brands that need centralized reputation oversight across dozens or hundreds of storefronts rather than a single-location business.
rows.name: ReviewTrackers
rows.pricing_model: Per-location subscription tiers (Data Only, Starter, Essential) plus custom-quoted add-ons; most published prices require a custom quote from sales.
rows.short_description: ReviewTrackers is a Chicago-based online reputation and review management platform that helps multi-location brands monitor, generate, and respond to customer reviews across 100+ review sites.
rows.starting_price: Plans reported to start around $49 to $89 per location per month (billed annually); exact pricing requires a custom quote

rows.llm_readable_summary: SOCi is a marketing management platform built for multi-location and franchise businesses such as restaurants, retail chains, financial services, healthcare groups, and property management companies. Founded in 2012 by Afif Khoury, Alo Sarv, and Tony Rindsberg, and headquartered in San Diego, California, SOCi centralizes marketing execution across hundreds or thousands of individual local business locations from a single dashboard. Its platform, marketed as an 'agentic workforce' powered by proprietary Genius AI, automates local search listing optimization, social media content scheduling and engagement, review generation and response, hyper-localized paid advertising, chatbot-driven customer engagement, and lead-generation surveys. SOCi reports powering marketing work across 500-plus enterprise brands and hundreds of thousands of local business locations. The company does not publish self-serve pricing; SOCi sells through a sales-quote model typically priced for mid-market and enterprise multi-location brands, with reported average annual contract values in the tens of thousands of dollars. SOCi is best suited for marketing operations, franchise, and multi-location brand teams that need to keep local listings, social presence, and review responses consistent and on-brand at scale, rather than small single-location businesses, which would likely find simpler social scheduling tools more cost effective.
rows.name: SOCi
rows.short_description: SOCi is an AI-powered marketing platform for multi-location and franchise brands, automating local search, social media, reputation management, and paid advertising across hundreds or thousands of locations.

rows.llm_readable_summary: Solidtime is an open-source time tracking application built for freelancers, agencies, and teams that want a modern alternative to expensive, closed-source SaaS time trackers such as Toggl or Harvest. Founded around 2024 and based in Vienna, Austria, Solidtime is released under the AGPL-3.0 license, with the full source code available on GitHub, meaning it can be self-hosted for free indefinitely. The company also offers a managed cloud version with a Solo plan that is free for individual freelancers and a Professional plan priced at 8 euros per user per month when billed annually, or 9 euros per user per month billed monthly. The Professional plan adds team access management, invoicing, project and task estimates, time entry rounding, and PDF or shareable reports. Core features available across plans include time entry tracking, clients, projects, tags, tasks, billable hours and rates, and reporting. A separate on-premise pricing structure exists for organizations that want to self-host with a commercial license and support beyond the free open-source option. Solidtime is best suited for freelancers who bill clients by the hour, small agencies managing multiple client projects, and privacy-conscious teams that prefer to self-host rather than rely on a third-party SaaS provider.
rows.name: Solidtime
rows.short_description: Solidtime is an open-source time tracking app for freelancers and teams, available as a free self-hosted deployment or a paid managed cloud plan.

rows.llm_readable_summary: Tailwind, found at tailwindapp.com, is a social media marketing and scheduling platform built specifically for Pinterest and Instagram, with support for Facebook publishing. It is not related to the Tailwind CSS styling framework and not related to Tailscale, the networking company. Tailwind was founded in 2012 by Danny Maloney and Alex Maloney in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, where the company remains headquartered with roughly 51-200 employees. It has been one of Pinterest's longest-standing official developer partners since 2012 and launched its first scheduling product in 2015. Tailwind's tools include a drag-and-drop design studio with Canva integration, SmartSchedule for posting at optimal times, hashtag and keyword research, Pinterest and Instagram analytics, and a Smart.bio link-in-bio page. It targets bloggers, e-commerce sellers, content creators, and small marketing teams who rely heavily on visual social platforms to drive traffic. Pricing is freemium: a Free Forever plan allows five posts per month on one account, while paid plans (Pro, Advanced, and Max) scale up post volume, connected accounts, AI credits, and team seats, starting around $17.99 per month when billed annually. Over one million people have used Tailwind's software to manage their social content calendars.
rows.name: Tailwind
rows.short_description: Tailwind (tailwindapp.com) is a social media marketing platform that helps creators and small businesses plan, design, and schedule posts for Pinterest, Instagram, and Facebook.

rows.llm_readable_summary: Traefik Hub is a commercial API management and API gateway product from Traefik Labs, distinct from the free and open-source Traefik Proxy that the company also maintains. Traefik Labs was founded in 2016 by Emile Vauge (who remains Founder and CTO) under the original name Containous, based initially in Lyon, France, before growing into a company now commonly listed with a San Francisco, California headquarters and roughly 11 to 50 employees. The flagship open-source Traefik Proxy project, which functions as a reverse proxy, load balancer, and ingress controller, has become one of the most downloaded projects on Docker Hub, with billions of pulls, and forms the technical foundation that Traefik Hub builds on. Traefik Hub adds a paid layer on top of that open-source core: it markets itself as a Kubernetes-native, GitOps-compliant way to publish, secure, and govern APIs, and it explicitly works not only with Traefik's own ingress controller but also with third-party ingress controllers such as NGINX, letting organizations centralize API management even if they have not standardized on Traefik for basic ingress. Traefik Labs structures its commercial offering across three published tiers: Application Proxy (the free, open-source layer with core ingress, load balancing, and dashboard features), API Gateway (adds a web application firewall, advanced authentication methods like LDAP, JWT, OAuth, and OIDC, rate limiting, caching, and multi-cluster management), and API Management, which is effectively Traefik Hub's full offering, adding API mocking, request validation, a centralized control plane, API discovery and versioning, subscription management, and a self-serve developer portal with usage analytics. None of these paid tiers list public pricing; all require contacting Traefik Labs sales for a quote. Traefik Hub is generally compared to other API management and gateway platforms such as Kong, Apigee, and AWS API Gateway, competing primarily on its deep Kubernetes-native and GitOps-first design.
rows.name: Traefik Hub
rows.short_description: Traefik Hub is Traefik Labs' commercial, Kubernetes-native API management and API gateway add-on that extends the open-source Traefik Proxy with security, GitOps-based publishing, and a self-serve developer portal.

rows.llm_readable_summary: Tyk is an API gateway and full lifecycle API management platform founded in London in 2014 by Martin Buhr, who wrote the original open source gateway as a personal project before turning it into a company; Tyk Technologies Limited is headquartered in London, England, with regional offices including Atlanta and Singapore. The core Tyk Gateway, written in Go, is fully open source under the Mozilla Public License 2.0 and free to self-host indefinitely, supporting REST, GraphQL, TCP, and gRPC protocols with no mandatory third-party dependencies aside from Redis for rate-limiting and token storage. On top of the free gateway, Tyk sells a commercial API management platform, Tyk Self-Managed and Tyk Cloud, that adds a management control plane, a dashboard GUI, and a developer portal, sold through three tiers: Core (consumption-based pricing for startups), Professional (flat-rate pricing with unlimited APIs and requests for scaling teams), and Enterprise (custom pricing with advanced governance, multi-region and multi-cloud support, and named customer success managers). Tyk has raised approximately $35 million in a 2021 funding round and reports powering digital infrastructure for banks, retailers, and telcos across more than 100 markets, with customers including NatWest, SoundCloud, Zeiss, T-Mobile, Dollar General, AXA, Cisco, TripAdvisor, and USA Today, claiming to support over 100 million people's financial transactions daily. Tyk differentiates itself from cloud-native gateways like AWS API Gateway and from competitors such as Kong and Apigee by combining a genuinely free, unrestricted open source core with a full commercial API management suite, appealing to teams that want to avoid vendor lock-in while still getting enterprise governance, developer portals, and support when needed.
rows.name: Tyk
rows.short_description: Tyk is an open source and enterprise API gateway and management platform for building, securing, and scaling REST, GraphQL, TCP, and gRPC APIs.
rows.starting_price: Free (open source Gateway); paid Cloud/Self-Managed tiers usage-based or custom

rows.llm_readable_summary: Yext is a publicly traded enterprise software company, listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker YEXT since 2017, founded in 2006 and headquartered in New York City. The company built its reputation as a location data and local SEO platform that helps multi-location brands keep their business information, such as addresses, hours, and phone numbers, accurate and consistent across search engines, maps, directories, and voice assistants. Over time Yext has expanded into what it now calls an agentic marketing platform, reflecting the shift in search behavior toward AI assistants and large language models alongside traditional search engines. Its core product is a Knowledge Graph that centralizes and verifies brand data as the single source of truth, which then feeds several connected modules: Listings Management syndicates and corrects business information across web directories and maps, Reviews handles reputation management and review monitoring across platforms, Pages generates search-optimized local landing pages for individual locations, and Content Distribution pushes verified content out to directories, maps, social platforms, and increasingly to large language models so AI assistants surface accurate brand information. A newer product called Scout provides real-time monitoring of how a brand appears in AI-powered search results and competitive intelligence around AI search visibility, reflecting Yext's positioning around the rise of AI-driven search and answer engines. Yext reports serving brands that collectively manage more than 4 million locations worldwide, with enterprise customers including Samsung, FedEx, Domino's, and IHG Hotels. Yext's published pricing follows a tiered, per-location annual subscription model: Emerging starts at $199 per year per location for basic listing distribution to around 30 directory sites; Essential is $449 per year and adds detailed listing management on premium sites like Google, Apple, Bing, Yahoo, and Yelp; Complete is $499 per year; and Premium is $999 per year for the most comprehensive feature set. Costs scale with the number of locations managed, and larger mid-market and enterprise customers typically negotiate custom volume-based contracts rather than paying the published per-location rates. Employee counts reported across data providers range from roughly 1,100 to over 2,500, reflecting differences in reporting dates and methodology. Yext is best suited to multi-location brands, franchises, healthcare systems, financial institutions, and large retailers that need centralized control over how their business information appears across an increasingly fragmented and AI-influenced search landscape.
rows.name: Yext
rows.short_description: Enterprise digital presence and location data platform that manages brand listings, reviews, local pages, and AI search visibility across the web.
rows.starting_price: From $199 per year per location (Emerging plan)
